2603.18683.title: HISR: Hindsight Information Modulated Segmental Process Rewards For Multi-turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
2603.18683.paper_summary: While large language models excel in diverse domains, their performance on complex longhorizon agentic decision-making tasks remains limited. Most existing methods concentrate on designing effective reward models (RMs) to advance performance via multi-turn reinforcement learning. However, they suffer from delayed propagation in sparse outcome rewards and unreliable credit assignment with potentially overly fine-grained and unfocused turnlevel process rewards. In this paper, we propose (HISR) exploiting Hindsight Information to modulate Segmental process Rewards, which closely aligns rewards with sub-goals and underscores significant segments to enhance the reliability of credit assignment. Specifically, a segment-level process RM is presented to assign rewards for each sub-goal in the task, avoiding excessively granular allocation to turns. To emphasize significant segments in the trajectory, a hindsight model is devised to reflect the preference of performing a certain action after knowing the trajectory outcome. With this characteristic, we design the ratios of sequence likelihoods between hindsight and policy model to measure action importance. The ratios are subsequently employed to aggregate segment importance scores, which in turn modulate segmental process rewards, enhancing credit assignment reliability. Extensive experimental results on three publicly benchmarks demonstrate the validity of our method.
2603.18683.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型在多個領域表現出色，但它們在複雜的長期代理決策任務上的表現仍然有限。大多數現有方法集中於設計有效的獎勵模型（RMs），通過多回合強化學習來提高性能。然而，它們在稀疏結果獎勵中遭遇延遲傳播的問題，並且在可能過於細緻且不集中的回合過程獎勵中面臨不可靠的信貸分配。在本文中，我們提出了(HISR)，利用後見信息來調節分段過程獎勵，這與子目標緊密對齊獎勵並強調重要的段落，以增強信貸分配的可靠性。具體而言，提出了一個段級過程RM，為任務中的每個子目標分配獎勵，避免對回合的過度細分分配。為了強調軌跡中的重要段落，設計了一個後見模型，以反映在知道軌跡結果後執行某個行動的偏好。基於這一特徵，我們設計了後見與策略模型之間的序列似然比率，以衡量行動的重要性。這些比率隨後被用來聚合段落重要性分數，進而調節分段過程獎勵，增強信貸分配的可靠性。在三個公共基準上的廣泛實驗結果證明了我們方法的有效性。
2603.18683.author: Zhicong Lu et.al.
2603.18683.authors: Zhicong Lu, Zichuan Lin, Wei Jia, Changyuan Tian, Deheng Ye, Peiguang Li, Li Jin, Nayu Liu, Guangluan Xu, Wei Feng
